title: Nova Scotia – Provincial corporation tax - Canada.ca
content:
## Nova Scotia – Provincial corporation tax
What's new for corporations
The lower rate of Nova Scotia income tax is 1.5% since April 1, 2025 . It was previously 2.5%.
The income eligible for the lower rate is determined using the $700,000 Nova Scotia business limit. Before April 1, 2025 , the limit was $500,000.
If the rate or the business limit changes during the tax year, you have to base your calculation on the number of days in the year that each rate or business limit is in effect.
The higher rate of income tax is 14% . The rate applies to taxable income earned in Nova Scotia that does not qualify for the lower rate.
These rates also apply to taxable income earned in the Nova Scotia offshore area.
For more information, see Dual tax rates .
You can use Schedule 346, Nova Scotia Corporation Tax Calculation , to help you calculate the Nova Scotia tax before the application of credits. You do not have to file it with the return. See the schedule for more details.
On line 215 and/or 220 of Schedule 5 Tax Calculation Supplementary – Corporations , enter the amount of tax calculated.
See also the details on the Nova Scotia financial institutions capital tax .

## Claiming the credits
Nova Scotia offers different tax credits. Details of each credit can be found in the following pages and their associated schedules:
- Capital investment tax credit
- Corporate tax reduction for new small businesses
- Digital animation tax credit
- Digital media tax credit
- Food bank tax credit for farmers
- Innovation equity tax credit
- Political contribution tax credit
- Research and development tax credit
- Venture capital tax credit

## Forms and publications
- Schedule 346, Nova Scotia Corporation Tax Calculation
- Schedule 5, Tax Calculation Supplementary – Corporations
- Nova Scotia (provincial site)
